AccoRevit.Addin
Table of Contents

Class FileDialogHelper

Namespace
AccoRevit.Addin.Utils
Assembly
AccoRevit.Addin.dll

Provides file and folder pickers that are safe to use from within Revit, with explicit owner-window handling to ensure dialogs appear in front of Revit and do not get lost behind the main application window.

public static class FileDialogHelper
Inheritance
object
FileDialogHelper
Inherited Members
object.ToString()
object.Equals(object)
object.Equals(object, object)
object.ReferenceEquals(object, object)
object.GetHashCode()
object.GetType()
object.MemberwiseClone()

Remarks

PickFile(FileType, string) primarily uses WPF's SaveFileDialog and falls back to WinForms SaveFileDialog if the WPF dialog fails (commonly due to windowing/owner issues).

PickFolder(string) uses CommonOpenFileDialog (Windows API Code Pack) because the WPF file dialogs do not provide a first-class folder picker.

The owner window/handle is resolved by first attempting the active WPF window, then Revit's main window handle via Nice3point Toolkit (when available), and finally Autodesk's Autodesk.Windows.ComponentManager.ApplicationWindow.

Methods

PickFile(FileType, string)

Prompts the user to select a destination file path for saving, using a file type-specific filter and default extension.

public static string PickFile(FileType fileType, string fileName = null)

Parameters

fileType FileType

The file type determining the filter and default extension.

fileName string

Optional file name (without path) to pre-populate in the dialog. If null or empty, no default name is applied.

Returns

string

The selected full file path if the user completes the dialog; otherwise an empty string.

Remarks

Uses SaveFileDialog as the primary UI. If an exception is thrown (typically due to UI owner/windowing constraints inside Revit), a WinForms SaveFileDialog fallback is used.

When possible, the dialog is shown with an explicit owner window/handle to keep it modal to Revit.

Exceptions

ArgumentException

Thrown when fileType is not present in the filter map.

PickFolder(string)

Prompts the user to select a destination folder.

public static string PickFolder(string initialDirectory = null)

Parameters

initialDirectory string

Optional initial directory to preselect when the dialog opens. If null or empty, the system default is used.

Returns

string

The selected folder path if the user completes the dialog; otherwise an empty string.

Remarks

Uses CommonOpenFileDialog configured for folder picking. When a Revit owner handle is available, it is used to keep the dialog properly parented to Revit.
